title: 7 Top Herbal Creams for Periodontal Disease Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin in Ginkgo Biloba is good for periodontal disease because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the gums, thereby preventing further damage and promoting healthy healing.

Tannins are good for periodontal disease because they have antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, thereby preventing the spread of infection and promoting healthy gums.
Catechin is good for periodontal disease because it helps reduce inflammation and fight bacteria that can cause gum disease and destroy teeth and gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia cream, also known as tea tree oil, is a natural remedy that can help with periodontal disease. This disease causes inflammation and infection in the gums, leading to pain and damage to the teeth and surrounding bone. The cream contains a compound called cymene, which has antibacterial properties. This means it can kill the bad bacteria in the mouth that cause periodontal disease. When applied to the gums, it reduces swelling and fights off the infection. It also helps to reduce the amount of plaque and tartar on the teeth, making it harder for bacteria to grow. Furthermore, the cream has antifungal properties, which can help to prevent fungal infections in the mouth that can make periodontal disease worse. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce pain and discomfort in the gums, making it a great natural remedy for periodontal disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal creams that help with periodontal disease, first, you need to prepare the Ginkgo biloba extract. Dry and grind 100 grams of Ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle. Then, mix one tablespoon of the Ginkgo biloba powder with one tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Next, add one tablespoon of coconut oil and one tablespoon of shea butter to the mixture, stirring constantly until the wax and oils are fully incorporated and melted. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature of around 120°F to 140°F. Once cooled, add a few drops of a natural preservative like vitamin E oil to extend the shelf life of the cream. Stir well and pour the mixture into small glass jars or containers. Let the cream cool and solidify completely before applying it to the gums to help soothe and prevent periodontal disease.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for periodontal disease

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosesmarinic acid is good for periodontal disease because it has strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce bacteria and prevent damage to gum tissue.
Quercetin is good for periodontal disease because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the production of enzymes that break down the bones and tissues in the gums.
Tannins are good for periodontal disease because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and prevent bacteria from growing in the gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, is a plant with many health benefits. It contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria, which are common causes of periodontal disease. This condition affects the gums and bone that support the teeth, causing pain and potentially leading to tooth loss. The antibacterial properties in sage can help control the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis. Additionally, sage has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the gums.  Sage has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various health issues, including oral health problems. Studies have shown that applying sage creams or oils to the gums can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. This can help prevent the progression of periodontal disease and promote healthy gums. The antimicrobial properties of sage can also help prevent infections and promote healing in the gums.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Ginkgo biloba herbal cream that helps with periodontal disease, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: Ginkgo biloba extract, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a preservative like vitamin E oil. Begin by melting the beeswax and carrier oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the mixture is smooth and melted, add in the Ginkgo biloba extract and stir well to combine. Next, remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before pouring it into a clean, sterilized container. Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before adding a few drops of vitamin E oil to act as a preservative. Once the cream is set, you can use it to help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums, making it a useful remedy for periodontal disease.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for periodontal disease

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for periodontal disease because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the gums, preventing further damage and promoting healthy gum tissue.
Tannins are good for periodontal disease because they have antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can cause inflammation and infections in the gums.

Quercetin is good for periodontal disease because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bacteria and inflammation in the gums, thus preventing further damage and promoting healthy gum tissue.
Caffeic acid is good for periodontal disease because it has strong antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that cause gum disease.
Alkylamides are good for periodontal disease because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and kill bacteria in the gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams may be good for periodontal disease because of its antibacterial properties. It contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid that can stop the growth of bad bacteria in the mouth. When bad bacteria build up, they can cause infections and damage gums. By reducing the number of bad bacteria, Echinacea purpurea creams may help to prevent or slow down periodontal disease. It may also reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, which can be a symptom of the disease. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea creams may boost the immune system, helping the body to fight off infections more easily.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal cream that helps with periodontal disease, you need to combine the herb with a carrier oil and a thickening agent. First, dry Ginkgo biloba leaves in a low-temperature oven or a dehydrator to remove moisture. Then, grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a spice grinder. Next, mix 2-3 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba powder with 1/4 cup of carrier oil, such as coconut oil or olive oil, in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the powder dissolves. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool. Once cooled, add 1-2 tablespoons of a thickening agent, such as beeswax or shea butter, to the mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated. Finally, pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool and thicken completely before using it as a cream to help with periodontal disease.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for periodontal disease

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for periodontal disease because it has strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bacteria growth and inflammation in the gums, thereby preventing further damage and promoting healthy gum tissue.
Catechins are good for periodontal disease because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the gums, which can help prevent and treat the condition.
Tannins are good for periodontal disease because they have antibacterial properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum inflammation and infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream is used to help treat periodontal disease. Periodontal disease is a problem where the gums and bone that hold the teeth in place become infected and damaged. This can lead to the teeth falling out. Eucalyptus globulus cream is good for this because it has special properties that help reduce inflammation and kill the bacteria that cause the infection. The eucalyptol in the cream is especially good at killing these bacteria and reducing swelling. When used regularly, the cream can help to stop the spread of the infection and promote healing in the gums. This can help to prevent the teeth from falling out and keep the mouth healthy. It can also help to reduce pain and discomfort caused by the infection.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Ginkgo biloba herbal cream that helps with periodontal disease, you will first need to gather your ingredients and equipment. Start by infusing Ginkgo biloba leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil by placing a handful of dried leaves in a clean glass jar and covering them with the oil. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the leaves infuse their goodness into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids. Next, you will need to add a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax to the infused oil to give it a creamy texture. Melt the wax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, then slowly add it to the infused oil, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens. Finally, add a preservative like vitamin E oil to extend the shelf life of your cream and pour it into a clean container. Your Ginkgo biloba herbal cream is now ready to use - simply apply it to the affected gums and massage it in to help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for periodontal disease

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Tannins help prevent periodontal disease by reducing the growth of bacteria in the mouth and preventing plaque from forming on teeth, thus reducing inflammation and the risk of gum disease.

Quercetin is good for periodontal disease because it helps to reduce inflammation, fight bacteria, and prevent bone loss, all of which are key factors in the development and progression of this condition.
Catechins are good for periodontal disease because they have strong antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the gums and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause the disease.
Polyphenols have been found to help prevent periodontal disease by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria in the mouth, thereby protecting the gums and teeth from damage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum, also known as clove oil, is often used in creams to help prevent and treat periodontal disease. This is because the active compound in clove oil, eugenol, has antibacterial properties that can kill the bacteria that cause gum inflammation and infection. When applied topically in a cream, eugenol can directly target the affected areas in the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. It can also help to reduce the severity of symptoms such as bad breath, bleeding gums, and pain while chewing. Additionally, clove oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it easier to manage periodontal disease. By using a Syzygium aromaticum cream, individuals can help to control the progression of the disease and prevent further damage to the gums and teeth.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal cream for periodontal disease, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, and a base cream like beeswax and shea butter. First, steep 2 tablespoons of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then let it cool. Next, strain the liquid and discard the leaves. Mix 1 cup of the cooled liquid with 1/2 cup of the carrier oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler. Heat the mixture until the beeswax melts, then remove from heat and stir in 2 tablespoons of shea butter until it's well combined. Finally, let the mixture cool and solidify, then scoop it into a container and use it as needed. This cream may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the gums, which can aid in the treatment of periodontal disease.
remedies.image_alt: Syzygium aromaticum herbal creams for periodontal disease

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is good for periodontal disease because it reduces inflammation and fights infection in the gums, thereby preventing the progression of the disease.

Demethoxycurcumin is good for periodontal disease because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, which can cause infection and damage to teeth and gums.

Polyphenols are good for periodontal disease because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bacteria and plaque buildup, and also improve blood flow to the gums, which can help prevent the progression of the disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams, also known as turmeric creams, have been found to be beneficial in treating periodontal disease. The main active compound in turmeric, called curcumin, has strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. This can lead to a decrease in bleeding and discomfort during brushing and flossing, making it easier to maintain good oral hygiene. Additionally, curcumin has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause periodontal disease. This can help prevent the progression of the disease and promote healing of the gums. Furthermore, curcumin has also been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation, fighting off bacteria, and protecting the gums from damage, Curcuma longa creams may be a valuable addition to a treatment plan for periodontal disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal creams that help with periodontal disease, you will need to combine the healing properties of this herb with a base cream. First, dry and grind 1-2 teaspoons of Ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix the Ginkgo powder with 2 tablespoons of a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a small bowl. Let the mixture sit for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, allowing the oil to extract the herbal properties from the Ginkgo powder. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another bowl. Then, mix 2-3 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of a base cream like aloe vera or shea butter in a separate bowl. Finally, apply the Ginkgo biloba cream to the affected gums 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and promote healing of periodontal disease.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for periodontal disease

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin in Ginkgo Biloba is good for periodontal disease because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause gum disease.

Isorhapontigenin is good for periodontal disease because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the gums, thereby preventing further damage and promoting healing.

Kaempferol is good for periodontal disease because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight bacteria in the gums, promoting healthy gum tissue and preventing further damage.
Bilobalide is good for periodontal disease because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and infection in the gums, promoting healthy gum tissue and preventing further damage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba cream is good for periodontal disease because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it easier to clean and maintain them. The cream also has antioxidant properties, which help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. Free radicals are unstable molecules that can harm healthy cells and tissues.  Ginkgo biloba cream contains flavonoids, which are plant compounds that have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. Improved blood flow helps to deliver more oxygen and nutrients to the gums, promoting healing and reducing the risk of infection. Flavonoids in the cream also have antibacterial properties, which help to fight off bacteria that can cause periodontal disease. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba cream can help to reduce symptoms of periodontal disease, such as bleeding gums and bad breath. It can also help to prevent the progression of the disease, reducing the risk of tooth loss and other complications. The cream is easy to apply and can be used in addition to regular oral hygiene practices, such as brushing and flossing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal creams that help with periodontal disease, you need to combine the healing properties of this herb with a base cream. First, dry and grind 1 part of Ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder. You can dry the leaves by placing them in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2-3 hours or by air-drying them for several days. Next, mix the Ginkgo powder with 2 parts of a base cream, such as coconut oil or shea butter, in a small bowl. Add a few drops of a carrier oil like jojoba oil to help the mixture blend smoothly. Heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring constantly, until the Ginkgo powder is fully incorporated into the cream. Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool. Once cooled, you can transfer the Ginkgo biloba cream into a small jar or container and store it in the refrigerator to preserve its potency. To use, apply a small amount of the cream directly to the affected gums or teeth twice a day. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Ginkgo biloba can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums, making it a potential remedy for periodontal disease.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for periodontal disease

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be beneficial for periodontal disease, a condition that affects the gums and bones supporting the teeth. Two herbs that have shown promise in treating periodontal disease are Melaleuca alternifolia and Salvia officinalis. Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has antimicrobial properties that help kill bacteria in the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Salvia officinalis, or sage, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. These herbs can be combined with other natural ingredients in a cream to create a treatment that can be applied directly to the affected area.  Periodontal disease is a serious issue that can lead to tooth loss and other health problems if left untreated. If you have periodontal disease, you may experience symptoms such as bleeding gums, bad breath, and loose teeth. If not treated, the infection can spread to other parts of the body, such as the heart and lungs. By treating periodontal disease, you can prevent these complications and improve your overall health.  Treating periodontal disease can improve your life in many ways. For one, it can help you maintain a healthy smile and prevent tooth loss. This can boost your self-confidence and make you feel more comfortable in social situations. Additionally, treating periodontal disease can help prevent other health problems, such as heart disease and diabetes. By taking care of your oral health, you can also reduce your risk of developing these conditions.
supplementary_best: A combination of herbal creams containing Melaleuca alternifolia, Curcuma longa, and Eucalyptus globulus is likely to be effective in treating periodontal disease. Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, contains compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the bacteria that cause gum infections. Curcuma longa, or turmeric, contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. Eucalyptus globulus, or eucalyptus oil, has decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. This combination is beneficial because it addresses multiple aspects of periodontal disease: the reduction of bacterial infection (Melaleuca alternifolia), the reduction of inflammation (Curcuma longa and Eucalyptus globulus), and the promotion of healing (Curcuma longa).
supplementary_avoid: If you have periodontal disease, you should avoid using creams that contain certain herbal ingredients. One such ingredient is Aloe vera, which can cause gum irritation and inflammation. This is because Aloe vera contains compounds that can stimulate the growth of new tissue, but they can also cause an allergic reaction in people with periodontal disease. Similarly, you should also avoid creams that contain Thyme, which can exacerbate gum inflammation. Thyme contains compounds that can increase the risk of infection and worsen the symptoms of periodontal disease. Additionally, creams that contain Rosemary should be avoided, as they can cause irritation to the gums and surrounding tissues. This is because Rosemary contains compounds that can cause blood vessels to constrict, leading to reduced blood flow to the gums and worsening of the condition. Finally, you should also be cautious when using creams that contain Calendula, as they can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions, which can be especially problematic for people with periodontal disease.
supplementary_other_remedies: Lozenges made from natural ingredients like aloe vera and vitamin C can help soothe mouth sores and reduce inflammation in the gums. These lozenges can be dissolved in the mouth, releasing their active ingredients to provide relief from pain and discomfort. Oil pulling with sesame oil has also been found to reduce plaque and prevent the formation of bacteria that can cause periodontal disease. The antimicrobial properties of sesame oil help to kill bacteria and prevent their growth in the mouth. Additionally, eating foods rich in omega-3 fatty acids, such as salmon and flaxseeds, can help reduce inflammation in the gums and prevent the progression of periodontal disease. The anti-inflammatory properties of omega-3 fatty acids help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums, promoting healthy gum tissue.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with periodontal disease are at higher risk of developing other health issues. One common problem is diabetes. Research shows that inflammation from gum disease can make it harder for the body to control blood sugar levels. Medicinal herbs like berberine and turmeric have anti-inflammatory properties that can help lower blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity. Berberine, in particular, has been shown to increase glucose uptake in the body's cells, making it easier to manage diabetes.  Another issue people with periodontal disease may experience is rheumatoid arthritis. Gum disease can trigger an overactive immune response, leading to inflammation in the body's joints. Herbs like ginger and boswellia have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and inflammation. Ginger, in particular, has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation by blocking chemical signals that promote pain.  People with periodontal disease are also at higher risk of heart disease and stroke. The inflammation from gum disease can damage blood vessels and increase the risk of blood clots. Herbs like omega-3 fatty acids and ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow. Omega-3 fatty acids, in particular, have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve heart health by reducing triglycerides and blood pressure.  Finally, people with periodontal disease may experience respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. The inflammation from gum disease can trigger an overactive immune response, leading to inflammation in the lungs. Herbs like licorice root and thyme have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and improve lung function. Licorice root, in particular, has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve lung function by reducing mucus production and improving airway function.
